notes: mistral-vibe `!`-shell escape. vibe runs `!echo hello` LOCALLY (no LLM turn) and writes ONE injected user line to messages.jsonl: `{"role":"user","injected":true,"content":"Manual `!` command result from the user. Use this as context only...."}` (confirmed in 3 live transcripts; source app.py:1574-1580 + agent_loop.py:751-777). As of fix 5e0b4f5c the vibe parser SKIPS injected:true user lines (parser.go:59-71: `if injected { ev.Skip=true; return ev }`), so the injected `!` result produces NO state change: the session stays in its prior state (ready) across the `!` window — no stray working flip, no stick in working. The final real prompt (injected:false) drives the ordinary ready→working→ready arc, proving the session is still alive. BACKFLOW (live basic-turn recording): mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, every later phase same_session_as session_birth (birth templated from the codex sibling). Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar, so model + tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<ts>_<hash> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)

After `!echo hello` runs locally, state stays ready — the injected `!` result is context-only and the parser skips it (parser.go:59-71, fix 5e0b4f5c), so no turn is synthesized and no stray working flip; the session stays in its prior state across the `!`.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt ('Reply with exactly the word: alive') drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
Final turn completes back to ready (assistant terminal text → turn_done → ready)
